new_rules.rule: All reviewer and leader outputs must pass one canonical contract-driven validator at the boundary before any workflow branch, state transition, or artifact write; malformed or incomplete payloads must fail closed.
new_rules.do: Parse the raw LLM JSON once, run it through a shared validator that checks required sections, allowed verdict/status vocabularies, field shapes, and leader arbitration invariants, then pass only the validated object into board logic.
new_rules.dont: Read `status` or `verdict` from raw parsed JSON and enter runtime branches while missing sections, wrong field types, or illegal arbitration combinations remain undetected.
new_rules.source: Round 4: partial runtime contract enforcement let malformed reviewer/leader payloads reach live workflow branches.

new_rules.rule: Top-level governed orchestrators must convert internal control exceptions into the declared typed terminal result and still emit required terminal artifacts; governed failure modes must not escape as uncaught exceptions.
new_rules.do: Catch `BoardBlockedError` at the `executeBoard` boundary, return the typed `{ status: 'blocked', ... }` result, and write `result.json` and `cycle-postmortem.json` from that terminal state.
new_rules.dont: Throw `BoardBlockedError` out of the round loop so the process exits before returning the declared result shape or writing terminal artifacts.
new_rules.source: Round 4: BoardBlockedError escaped `executeBoard`, breaking the typed return contract and skipping terminal artifacts.

existing_rules_covering.explanation: COD-003 already requires canonical review data and audit evidence to be preserved end-to-end without lossy remapping. Reusing the pre-override leader `unresolved` array after the board computed a different effective verdict creates exactly that evidence drift; the reconciled post-override unresolved set must be the only persisted version everywhere.

COD-004 already says shared governed workflow logic must have one canonical implementation and all live consumers must import it. Duplicating `pathExists` and `buildAuditEnvelope` in `board.ts` and `index.ts` is the mirror-module drift this rule was written to prevent.

COD-003 covers preservation of canonical provenance through machine-facing handoffs. Hard-coding 'leader LLM' into every auto-fix prompt rewrites the true failing participant and degrades the provenance passed to the fixer, so the context label must preserve the actual source role.
